publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-09-27 00:09:43 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Manuscripts, letters, documents, photographs, bound manuscripts, galley proofs, and broadsides.
did.abstract: Manuscripts, letters, documents, photographs, bound manuscripts, galley proofs, and broadsides by, to or about James Joyce, mainly in the early years of his writing career. Manuscripts include the earliest known manuscript of his first book of poems, Chamber Music; a typescript of the first sketch of Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man; an essay containing an important early statement of Joyce's literary theory; and manuscripts for parts of Dubliners, Stephen Hero, Ulysses, Pomes Penyeach, and his play Exiles.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts, letters, documents, photographs, bound manuscripts, galley proofs, and broadsides by, to or about James Joyce, mainly in the early years of his writing career. Manuscripts include the earliest known manuscript of his first book of poems, Chamber Music; a typescript of the first sketch of Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man; an essay containing an important early statement of Joyce's literary theory; and manuscripts for parts of Dubliners, Stephen Hero, Ulysses, Pomes Penyeach, and his play Exiles.

Letters include substantial correspondence within the Joyce family; substantial correspondence between Joyce and his lover Nora Barnacle, later Nora Joyce, including erotic love letters from Joyce to her; and some fifty letters from Ezra Pound. Also included are correspondence and documents relating to the publication of Joyce's work, with his literary agent James B. Pinker, his patron Harriet Shaw Weaver, publishers Elkin Mathews and Grant Richards, and others. Other correspondents include Oliver St. John Gogarty; Monro, Saw & Co., the law firm which administered Harriet Shaw Weaver's patronage of Joyce; John Quinn, and W.B. Yeats.

arrangement.head: COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
arrangement.p: Organized into these series: Manuscripts by Joyce; letters by him; letters by others to or about Joyce; photographs and unidentified and miscellaneous material.
Manuscripts arranged alphabetically by title; letters by Joyce, in chronological order; letters by others, alphabetically by author.

arrangement.head: Series List
arrangement.p: Series I. Manuscripts Boxes 1-2, and bound manuscripts
Series II. Documents and Miscellaneous Box 3
Series III. Outgoing Correspondence March 1901-December 1905 - Box 4July 1906-August 1911 - Box 5April 1912-January 1941 - Box 6
Series IV. Incoming Correspondence, Manuscripts and Documents Boxes 7-14
Series V. Photographs and Miscellaneous Box 15

odd.head: General
odd.p: Abreviations: AL Autographed Letter, AM Autographed Manuscript, AD Autographed Document, D Document, DS Document Signed, TM Typed Manuscript.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: James Joyce collection, #4609. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access restricted to the permission of the curator.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Researchers may view one folder at a time.

did.unittitle: Autograph copies of two songs by Ben Jonson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Copies on recto and verso of single slip of paper. Song 3 from The Metamorphos'd Gypsies and "Give end unto thy pastimes, Love" Scholes 19.

did.unittitle: "La Battaglia fra Bernard Shaw e la Censura. Blanco Posnet Smascherato"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 leaves. Written in Trieste. With two copies of English translation. Published in Il Piccolo della Sera Sept. 5, 1909. Typescript dated August 31. Scholes 39.

did.unittitle: Review of The Bruno Philosophy by J. Lewis McIntyre

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 leaves. Typed transcripts from The Daily Express of October 30, 1903 in two copies. Scholes 1387.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 36 leaves. In final or nearly final form; some poems bearing pencil emendations by Joyce. Includes four poems not included in the final text. In the hands of James Joyce, Stanislaus Joyce [?], and perhaps another hand. Cf. Scholes 12. Scholes 21.

did.unittitle: Fragment of Poem XXI of Chamber Music

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in The Tower, Sandycove. On stationary of "The Tower, Sandycove." Scholes 23.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Third page of a story by James Joyce. A fair copy on one leaf of wove paper. First line reads "by on Wellington Quay. They used often to meet at Swans." Scholes 29.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 leaves. Written in Galway City. Published in Il Piccolo della Sera Aug. 11, 1912. With typescript English translation. Scholes 39.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 leaves. Written in Trieste. Published in Il Piccolo della Sera Dec. 20, 1910. With partial English translation. Scholes 39.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. A fragment of a song set to music by James Joyce. Five bars of words and music of a song, in Joyce's hand on paper ruled for musical composition. Scholes 60.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 56 leaves. Manuscript begins as a typescript and is completed in the hand of an amanuensis. Contains some corrections in James Joyce's hand. Scholes 31.

did.unittitle: "Ivy Day in the Committee Room" from Dubliners

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 leaves. Autograph manuscript with notations for insertions of printed slips--cf. Scholes 33. Not in Scholes

did.unittitle: Printed slip with passage from "Ivy Day in the Committee Room"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Slips like this were printed by Joyce for inclusion in a circular letter to the press written during his dispute with Maunsel over Dubliners. The first sentences read "--But look here, John, --said Mr O'Connor. --Why should we welcome the king of England? Didn't Parnell himself?" Scholes 33.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 15 leaves. This is the text of the Dubliners version of the story, not of the shorter Irish Homestead version. This autograph manuscript was probably used to set up the Maunsel edition of Dubliners, but the final text of "The Sisters" is slightly different from this manuscript. Scholes 30.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 36 leaves. In various hands, including that of James Joyce. Scholes 51.

did.unittitle: Transcript of Italian review of Dubliners

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Scholes 51.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 leaves. Essentially notations, arranged chronologically, ranging from drama's "Religious origins" in the Greek and Hindu traditions to "Elizabethan Drama." For use in a lecture? Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: "An Epiphany"- dialogue between Oliver St. John Gogarty and a shop assistant

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Dublin. 1 leaf. An autograph manuscript on one side of a leaf of good-quality laid paper. A rough draft with corrections and blots. Scholes 18.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 22 small leaves. Enclosed in an envelope. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 leaves. Written in Trieste. One of a series of articles by Joyce published in Il Piccolo della Sera. With partial English translation of Italian text. Scholes 39.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Autograph manuscript in pencil on the first page of a ruled piece of wove paper. There are some changes in the first line. A poem of three quatrains. Scholes 50.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. An early draft, probably the first, of this broadside by James Joyce. An autograph manuscript in pencil beginning with the line, "To show you for strictures I don't care a button." The title, "Falconer addresses the Vigilance Committee," appears on the right-hand margin. The text is on the verso of the second leaf of a printed form of an empty royalty agreement from Maunsel and Co., Ltd., on a wove double-leaf. Scholes 46.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. "Written in the train between Flushing and Salzburg." A later draft of this broadside. Autograph manuscript in pencil with ink corrections of the entire 98 line poem. The paper used for this draft was originally a typed copy of an agreement (unsigned) between Joyce and the Maunsel firm. Scholes 47.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Flushing. Fair copy of the broadside by James Joyce. An autograph manuscript of the entire poem in ink in an especially legible hand. There are a few changes in ink in the text of the poem. Scholes 48.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Trieste. 1 leaf. This broadside was printed in Trieste in an edition of 100 copies. 40 of these were sent to Charles Joyce for distribution in Dublin. This photostat shows a hand-written note added in ink by Joyce dated July 1912.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Autograph manuscript written on one side of a leaf of white wove paper. Corrections are in ink in the left margin. In the upper corner is the number 11 in pencil. This is the page 11 that is missing from Slocum E17a and from the published version in Critical Writings. Scholes 42.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 leaves. Written in Trieste. A typed transcript of manuscript fragments of a lecture by James Joyce on the Irish poet Mangan, delivered in Italian in Trieste, with carbon copy. Scholes 41.

did.unittitle: Transcripts of critical material relating to Shakespeare's Hamlet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 62 pages on 60 leaves. Written in Trieste. Likely for preparation of a series of lectures by Joyce on Hamlet in 1912. This autograph manuscript consists largely of historical and literary excerpts to be used in reference to specific acts and scenes of the play. Some material is marked for use in an introduction and a conclusion. Two pages consist of notes that seem separate from the excerpts. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Trieste. Fragment of manuscript with printer's slip attached. This autograph manuscript is the last page of the final draft, numbered "4" in pencil. It is a fair copy made for the Triestine printer on white wove paper. Pasted to the top of the manuscript is the slip of the printer, L. Smolars, with the notation (among others) that 100 copies were to be printed. Scholes 26.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 leaves. Written in Trieste. Text, in Italian, of article Joyce published in Il Piccolo della Sera, with English translation. Scholes 39.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 leaves. Written in Trieste. Text, in Italian, of article published by Joyce in Il Piccolo della Sera, with English translation. Scholes 39.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. In Italian, with carbon copy. The lecture was delivered in Trieste, in March 1912. The typescript itself dates from much later. Scholes 44.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. List in James Joyce's hand of 51 volumes on "Shelf 3: Front" and 32 volumes on "Shelf 3: Back." Scholes 1400.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 leaves. Written in Galway. Text, in Italian, with English translation, of an article published by Joyce in the Sept. 5, 1912 edition of Il Piccolo della Sera. Scholes 39.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Written in Zurich. Doggerel verses on the Henry Carr incident. 2 carbon copies. Scholes 1410.

did.unittitle: Notebook containing translation into Italian of Chapters 1 and 2 of George Moore's Celibates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 55 leaves. With corrections in another hand. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Notebook listing four dreams, with interpretations of three of them by Joyce.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Four dreams, apparently dreamt by Nora Joyce. Joyce dates the first dream as in the tercentenary of Shakespeare's death [1916]. This autograph manuscript of James Joyce is in a copybook bound in half cloth tucked under flimsy blue cardboard. The dreams are numbered 1-4 in red-orange crayon. The fourth dream lacks interpretation. Scholes 52.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Material for Piccolo della Sera articles or lecture, "L'Irlanda Isola dei Santi e dei Savi" delivered in Trieste in 1912. Scholes 43.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20 leaves. Written in Trieste. A fragment of a draft, possibly final, of James Joyce's lecture on Blake, delivered in Italian in Trieste. This autograph manuscript consists of 2 unbound booklets of wove paper, each booklet made up of 10 leaves, sewn with a single gray thread. A translation of this manuscript in included in Critical Writings. Scholes 46.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 leaves. One page is written in English, the other two are in Italian. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 leaves. Written in Trieste. Published in Il Piccolo della Sera. With 2 English translations. Scholes 39.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 leaves. Written in Trieste. Text, in Italian, with English translation, of an article published by Joyce in Il Piccolo della Sera. Scholes 39.

did.unittitle: Eight poems which were included in Pomes Penyeach

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 29 leaves. Typescripts and carbon copies of the following 8 poems: "Cabra," an early version of "Tilly"; "Watching the Needleboats at San Sabba"; "A Flower Given to My Daughter"; "She Weeps over Rahoon"; "Tutto e sciolto," an early version; "Simples"; "Flood"; "Nightpiece." Scholes 54.

did.unittitle: Fragment of A Portrait of the Artist

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. This manuscript in the hand of Stanislaus Joyce includes the first part of the narrative. This copy is on 3 sides of 2 leaves of paper. The narrative is continued only into the second paragraph and then abandoned. Scholes 35.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 leaves. A typescript of this first brief sketch of James Joyce's novel. January 7, 1904 is the date of the autograph manuscript from which this typescript was made, but the copy itself was made by or for Stanislaus Joyce ca. 1928. The main outlines of the later work--and even some of the incidents--are here in this sketch of January 1904. Scholes 34.

did.unittitle: "The Right Heart in the Wrong Place"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Paris, with envelope addressed to Stanislaus Joyce. Poem is written on one side of a plain card, signed "S.O.S." Scholes 59.

did.unittitle: "The Right Man in the Wrong Place"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Paris. Poem is written on one side of a plain card, signed "J.J." Scholes 59.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. A Latin poem in James Joyce's hand on 2 paper slips of the size used by the National Library in Ireland. Scholes 1443.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 leaves. Material for lecture delivered in 1912? A chronological listing of Shakespeare's activities to 1606. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 leaves. This autograph manuscript consists of 5 leaves of thin white wove paper, the versos blank except for the large page numbers: 479, 480, 490, 498, 506. These pages are missing from the Yale manuscript of a chapter of Stephen Hero (Slocum E3bii) and from the edition published by New Directions (New York, 1955). Scholes 36.

did.unittitle: Telegraphic code prepared by Joyce, probably in connection with his Volta Theatre project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Autograph manuscript on one page of wove paper. "Codice" is written at the top of the page. On the left side of the recto is a list of code words in Italian. Opposite each of these words is a phrase or sentence for which the word is to stand. The code is continued on the upper half of the verso. Scholes 40.

did.unittitle: Translation into Italian of a poem by James Stephens, "Stephen's Green" [copy]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Carbon copy of James Stephen's poem and an Italian translation of it by James Joyce. "James Stephens" typed at the end of the poem, "James Joyce" typed at the end of the translation. The title of the translation has been corrected in pencil by James Joyce to "Il Vento (nel parco di Stephen, Dublino)." Scholes 62.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Belvedere College. One of the weekly compositions written by James Joyce for George Dempsey's class in English at Belvedere College. An autograph essay on the first 3 pages of a ruled double-leaf torn from the center of a copybook. At the top of each page is the motto "AMDG"; at the bottom of the last page ".LSD." Scholes 1.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. A list of characters for the "Cyclops" chapter. Autograph manuscript on the back of a letter from Henry Davray, dated June 23, 1919, on blue stationary of the Anglo-French Review. It is written in pencil, with the title "Cyclops" in a circle at the top of the page. Scholes 55.

did.unittitle: Typescript of the "Nausicaa" episode of Ulysses [copy]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 leaves. Copy of typescript in box 2, folder 22. Scholes 57.

did.unittitle: Typescript of the "Nausicaa" episode of Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 leaves. A complete draft, close to the published version; with autograph corrections in the author's hand. There are some corrections and changes in ink in James Joyce's hand. The manuscript consists of 21 leaves numbered in pencil, 1-9, 9a, 10-20. Scholes 57.

did.unittitle: Draft of the "Nausicaa" episode of Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 16 leaves. A draft of the last half of the "Nausicaa" chapter. This autograph manuscript of James Joyce is contained in two identical notebooks with covers of thick purple paper. Both notebooks have blank labels pasted on their front covers. The labels are decorated with identical red borders except that the label of the first notebook has a red anchor and the letters "C G" worked into the lower margin. Scholes 56.

did.unittitle: Fragment of "Oxen of the Sun" episode from Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 leaves. This autograph manuscript of James Joyce contains a draft of the end of the "Oxen of the Sun" chapter with corrections in the left margins. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Two fragments of music- "Whittington Chimes" and "Ca ira"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. These two fragments of music are in James Joyce's hand on the recto of the first leaf and the verso of the second leaf of a double-leaf of paper ruled for musical composition. The paper bears a printed design of a lion on a branch holding a shield with two stars and the name "ZURICH" over the notation "SCHUTZMARKE No. 8." Scholes 61.

did.unittitle: Embryological chart for "Oxen of the Sun" episode of Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 large leaf. Drawn on one side of a leaf of wove paper are nine numbered ovals of different sizes representing the monthly stages of gestation. The ovals are in red ink, beginning with the smallest almost at the lower edge of the page, each successive oval including its predecessors, the last almost reaching the top of the page. At the apices of seven of the ovals are notations about the related stage in fetal development, written in ink in James Joyce's hand. The apex of the sixth oval is distinguished by a star in red ink. In the upper left corner in a large parenthesis is the notation "Oxen of the Sun." Scholes 58.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes annotations and transcription.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A series of notes kept by James Joyce on the following subjects: Byrne (John Francis), Cosgrave (Vincent), Clancy (George Stephen), Casey (Joseph), Calvacanti [sic] (Guido), Dedalus (Stephen), Devin, Esthetic, England, Gogarty (Oliver Saint John), Gordon (Michael), Giorgino, Henry (Father William), Healy (Michael), Ireland, Jesus, Ibsen (Henrik), Jesuits, Lust, Mother, McCluskey, Nora Pappie, Prezioso (Roberto), Poppie, Roucati (Venanzio), Rogers, (Marcellus), Skeffington (Francis Joseph Christopher), Sordina (Conte Francesco), Shelley (Percy Bysshe), Uncle William, and Walshe (Louis). These autograph notes are contained in a thick notebook, bound in half cloth over red-and-black mottled boards, with marbled edges. There are 300 ruled leaves in the book. The leaves are divided by 25 red tabs with white letters and have been cut so as to display the tabs, which are arranged in alphabetical order from top to bottom. Much of the material in this book was used verbatim by James Joyce in his published works, especially Ulysses and Portrait. Scholes 25.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A notebook on banking kept by James Joyce. These autograph writings are in a notebook, bound in half cloth over red-brown-and-white boards, with marbled edges. On the recto of the first page after the unruled end paper is the title, "Banking." The book consists of 138 ruled leaves. The book begins with the history of banking and continues through such subsections as "Clerical Work" and "The Stock Exchange." Scholes 38.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also on microfilm. A journal kept by Stanislaus Joyce. In this journal, Stanislaus Joyce used for his own purposes paper which had been previously used - mainly by James Joyce - for other purposes. The manuscript consists of 202 leaves and contains in addition to this journal the other manuscripts and fragments described as Scholes 5 (Miscellaneous fragments), 6 (Fragment of an essay, on subjugation), 7 (The study of languages), 8 (Royal Hibernian Academy: "Ecce Homo"), 9 (The Apocalypse of Saint John), 10 (Drama and Life), 11 (Medical notes), 12 "I said I will go down to where"), 13 (From Chamber Music, two early drafts of the opening lines of poem XXXVI), 14 (Fragment of an essay, on the French attempt to build an empire in North Africa), 15 (Three epiphanies: a) "Her arm is laid for a moment on my knees." b) "Faintly, under the heavy summer night." c) "She comes at night when the city is still."). Scholes 4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 32 leaves. Galley proofs of the Grant Richards edition. These proofs are printed on sheets of poor-quality laid paper. They begin with page 3 of the book and continue through page 137. This is evidently an extra set of proofs, not used for corrections. Scholes 53.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: James Joyce's workbook in Italian and German. These autograph writings are in a large notebook, bound in half cloth over brown-and-black boards, with marbled edges. On the front cover is pasted a large label with the word "Italiano" in Joyce's hand. The book consists of 138 leaves of unruled white wove paper. The first part of the notebook is a workbook in Italian combined with a commonplace book in Italian. There are 16 leaves of German-English vocabulary before the last, blank, leaf. Scholes 37. ca. 1904 and continued for several years.

did.unittitle: Advance notice of an exhibition and sale of books and mss. left by James Joyce to be held at The Librairie-Galerie

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sale notice is typed on 6 large leaves. It contains a detailed description of sale items separated into four categories: Manuscript notes, the Works of Joyce, A Working Library, and Family Portraits. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A series of random jottings. This autograph manuscript is in a notebook, bound in half cloth over red-blue-and-gray-marbled boards with marbled edges. The book consists of 100 ruled leaves partially numbered by James Joyce for the "Trade" section of his notes on banking (see Bd. Ms. 2 and Scholes 38). The recto of the unruled end paper bears the title "Trade" and the rectos of the following 6 pages, numbered 1-6, contain James Joyce's notes on this subject. Stanislaus's jottings begin on the recto of the eighth leaf and are continued for 66 pages on 33 leaves. Scholes 63.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ithaca. On tape. A lecture given at the Cornell Festival of Contemporary Arts. Not in Scholes. Formerly listed as 4609 Bd Ms+ 115, maybe in collection #11-8-771.

did.unittitle: Drawing of coat of arms of Ireland, with commentary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pen and ink drawing of the Arms of Ireland with notes, possibly in James Joyce's hand. Scholes 1439.

did.unittitle: Listing of articles published in Il Piccolo della Sera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. A list of Joyce's contributions to this Triestine journal. The list of articles, in Stanislaus Joyce's hand, is on one side of a leaf of white wove paper, with scalloped upper edge. Scholes 39.

did.unittitle: Title page (paper cover) of Ibsen's Digte, Copenhagen, 1891 autographed by Joyce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. This title page from Ibsen's Digte is signed "JasAJoyce" in upper right corner. Scholes 1437.

did.unittitle: Printed form announcing the enclosure of a cheque to James Joyce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in 11 Henrietta Street, London. 1 leaf. To James Joyce, a printed form announcing the enclosure of a cheque (for 12/12/0), for his contribution to the Fortnightly Review for April 1900. Signed Hayward. Scholes 511.

did.unittitle: Unidentified fragment of a letter addressed to Joyce by a friend of Lady Gregory.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Paris. 1 leaf. Fragment of a letter from a friend of Lady Gregory to James Joyce, offering advice on living in Paris. Scholes 1447.

did.unittitle: Matriculation certificate, Royal University of Ireland

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Dublin. 1 leaf. Matriculation certificate of the Royal University of Ireland. States that James Joyce passed the Matriculation Examination in June 1899 and obtained second-class honors in Latin. Signed J.A.Joyce." on verso. Scholes 1374.

did.unittitle: Ticket admitting James Joyce to the conferring of degrees, Royal University of Ireland. [card]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Dublin. 1 leaf. Ticket to a public meeting of the Royal University of Ireland for the conferring of degrees. Scholes 1381.

did.unittitle: Clipping from Irish Times on Feis Ceoil singing competition [newspaper clipping]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Dublin. 1 leaf. Clipping from the Irish Times of an article on the Feis Ceoil singing competition in which Joyce took third place. The paragraphs that mention Joyce are marked with a box. Scholes 1388.

did.unittitle: Telegram to Joyce, from unidentified sender reading "Impossible get advance"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Dublin. 1 leaf. "Joyce" written on verso in blue crayon. Scholes 1449.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clipping advertising instruction in English at the Berlitz School, taught by James Joyce, Il Giornaletto di Pola

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Pola. 1 leaf. Scholes 1389.

did.unittitle: Contract with Grant Richards for publication of Dubliners

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Trieste. 1 leaf. Signed by Grant Richards. Scholes 1394.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Trieste. 1 leaf. Scholes 1376.

did.unittitle: Bill relating to the birth of Lucia Joyce, Ospitale Civico, Trieste

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Trieste. I leaf. Scholes 1414.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Contract in Italian for the Volta Theatre project of James Joyce, Giuseppe Caris, Giovanni Rebez, Caterina Machnich (a typed copy, unsigned, undated). Scholes 1404.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Galway [?]. "Naming no names, but obviously the story of James and Nora and their flight to the Continent."- George Healey. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Contract with Maunsel and Co. for publication of Dubliners. Signed George Roberts and James Joyce. Scholes 1395.

did.unittitle: Listing of expenses for the Volta Theatre

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. List in James Joyce's hand of expenses for the Volta Theatre. Dated December, January, February. Scholes 1405.

did.unittitle: Theatre Programme announcement of forthcoming publication of Dubliners, Abbey Theatre, Dublin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 leaves. Written in Dublin. Announcement reads, " DUBLINERS, By JAMES JOYCE. 3s. 6d. Net. (Ready in September)." Scholes 1382.

did.unittitle: Programma per il XII anno accademico, Universita Popolare Triestina, Instituto municipale [program]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 leaves. Written in Trieste. Program of the Universita Popolare Triestina for its twelfth academic year (November 5, 1911 to March 31, 1912) in which are scheduled two lectures by Prof. J. Joyce: "Verismo ed idealismo nella letteratura inglese (Daniele De Foe-William Blake). Scholes 1383.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Trieste. Scholes 1378.

did.unittitle: "A Bi-Centenary Ode" by Oliver St. John Gogarty, [newspaper clipping]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Written in Dublin. Clipping from an unidentified newspaper of an article on the bicentenary celebrations of Trinity College Medical School and an ode written for the occasion by Oliver St. John Gogarty. Scholes 1390.

did.unittitle: Report cards from the Civica Scuola Popolare E Cittadina in Trieste

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Written in Trieste. Scholes 1379.

did.unittitle: Printed ticket of admission to a series of ten lectures in English by Joyce on "Amleto di G. Shakespeare"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Trieste. Scholes 1384.

did.unittitle: Extracts from press notices and reviews of Dubliners [pamphlet]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 leaves. Grant Richards, London. 3 copies (2 green, same font, 1 pink, different font, but identical text). Scholes 1391.

did.unittitle: Contract with Grant Richards for the publication of Dubliners

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Trieste. Signed by Grant Richards. Scholes 1396.

did.unittitle: Bill sent to James Joyce from Triestine bookseller, F.H. Schimpff

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Trieste. Bill listing twenty books purchased by James Joyce from September 1913 to May 1914. Scholes 1401.

did.unittitle: Memorandum of agreement making J.B. Pinker agent for James Joyce's literary work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Trieste. Signed by James Brand Pinker. Scholes 1398.

did.unittitle: Memorandum of agreement making J.B. Pinker agent for Joyce's dramatic work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Trieste. Signed by James Brand Pinker. Scholes 1397.

did.unittitle: Extracts from press notices and reviews of A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man [pamphlet]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 leaves. Written in London, New York. The four pamphlets are entitled, "Extracts from Continental Press Notices," "Extracts from American Press Notices," "Extra Press Notices," and "Extract from Press Notices." Scholes 1391.

did.unittitle: Listing of periodicals in which reviews of A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man appeared

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. A list of 11 periodicals in which reviews of Portrait appeared. Dated February to September 1917. Scholes 1440.

did.unittitle: Contract with Grant Richards for the publication of Exiles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Written in Zurich. Signed by Grant Richards. With typed carbon copy of extra clauses inserted by James Joyce in the "Exiles" agreement. Scholes 1399.

did.unittitle: Extracts from press notices and reviews of Exiles [pamphlet]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 leaves. Written in London, New York. Scholes 1391.

did.unittitle: Group of papers relating to the 1918-1919 lawsuit between Joyce and Henry Carr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 leaves. Written in Zurich. In German. Scholes 1409.

did.unittitle: Notice of proclamation of Irish Republic [newspaper clipping]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Zurich. In German. Scholes 1392.

did.unittitle: Copies of statements signed by Joyce regarding the lawsuit between Joyce and Henry Carr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20 leaves. Written in Zurich. Four copies in English, two in German. Scholes 1408.

did.unittitle: Bill sent to James Joyce from Leipzig bookseller, Simmel and Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Leipzig. Bill listing five books purchased by James Joyce from "antiquarisch aus Katalog 229." Scholes 1402.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Clipping from Il Mondo of an article on James Joyce by F. Paolo Giordani. Scholes 1393.

did.unittitle: Printed announcement of a lecture to be given by Valery Larbaud

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Paris. Sponsored by La Maison des Amis des Livres for Joyce's benefit on Dec. 7. Scholes 1385.

did.unittitle: Typed listing of signatures protesting Samuel Roth's pirating of Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Paris. Some names added in ink. Envelope addressed to Stanislaus Joyce in James Joyce's hand. Scholes 1411.

did.unittitle: Typed list of signatures protesting Samuel Roth's pirating of Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Paris. Additional names added in ink; with envelope addressed to Stanislaus Joyce in James Joyce's hand. List longer than that of box 3, folder 39 above. Scholes 1412.

did.unittitle: Printed listing of signatures, with statement of intent, protesting Samuel Roth's pirating of Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Paris. Printed protest including printed list of 167 signatures. Scholes 1413.

did.unittitle: Typed report on the condition of Joyce's eyes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in Paris. with envelope. Typed report on the results of an exam of his eyes, sent by James Joyce to Stanislaus Joyce. Scholes 1442.

did.unittitle: Printed program for concert, Morley College Concert Society

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Written in London. Among selections is composition by Matyas Seiber, "Ulysses" for solo tenor, chorus and orchestra, based on texts by Joyce. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 54 leaves. Notes and chapters relating to a thesis on Italian influences on James Joyce, probably prepared under the direction of Stanislaus Joyce, with some corrections in his hand and some in an unidentified hand. Scholes 1445.

did.unittitle: Publisher's announcement re forthcoming publication of Ellmann's biography, James Joyce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Entitled "Why this is the book on James Joyce." Includes order form. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For production by Olney Theatre, Olney, Maryland. Gift of Giles F. Shepherd, Aug. 16, 1966. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Newspaper clipping from N.Y. Times announcing death of Mary Kathleen (Joyce) Monaghan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Program of the First James Joyce Symposium. Dublin. Ireland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Dublin. 2 copies. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For readings from Joyce adapted by Dan Laurence and presented at Incarnate Word College Reader's Theatre. With news clipping on the production from San Antonio Express-News, Feb. 28, 1976. Gift of Bernard F. Burgunder. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Oliver St. John Gogarty (1 copy of a letter)
Henrik Ibsen (1 draft of a letter)
Secretary of the Academy of Music (1 letter)
Letter "to the editor" regarding Dubliners (1 letter)
William A. Bradley (l letter, between July and December 1920)
Sir Horace Rumbold, British Minister (1 copy)
Series IV. Incoming Correspondence, Manuscripts and Documents
Rejection slip for Dubliners, from Edward Arnold
Mrs. Thomas Barnacle to Nora Joyce (3 letters)
Sylvia Beach to Stanislaus Joyce (5 letters)
City of Dundee Prisoners of War Help Committee
Sir Arthur Clutton-Brock to Ezra Pound (1) and to James Joyce (2)
Memorandum from the Consorzio Industriale di Mutui Prestiti (4)
Alphabetical name index, G - Joyce, Helen
George V., King of Great Britain via his Private Secretary
Bill from London bookseller, Henry W. Glover
Oliver St. John Gogarty (24 letters & documents)
Sir Edmund William Gosse to W.B. Yeats (1) and James Joyce (1)
Great Britain, Consulate General, Zurich to James Joyce (4) to C.W. Sykes (2)
Felix E. Hackett to Miss Power
Edmund J Healy to Oliver St. John Gogarty [copy]
Incorporated Society of Authors, Playwrights and Composers (4)
Incorporated Stage Society to Mr. Sturge Moore
Helen Kastor Joyce to Stanislaus Joyce (2)
Alphabetical name index, Joyce, John Stanislaus - Joyce, Nora
John Stanislaus Joyce to James Joyce (5)
John Stanislaus Joyce to Stanislaus Joyce (22)
Documents relative to registration of birth of Lucia Joyce
Lucia Joyce to Mrs. Herbert Hughes (3)
Margaret Alice Joyce (Poppie) to Stanislaus Joyce (6)
Nora Joyce to Mrs. Herbert Hughes (2)
Alphabetical name index, Joyce, Stanislaus - K
Notebook, "Selections in Prose from Various Authors," Stanislaus Joyce
Notebook, "Selections in Verse from Various Authors," Stanislaus Joyce
Notes on English Literature, Stanislaus Joyce
Mary S. Kettle to Israel Kaplan
Little, Brown and Co. to John Quinn
David Lloyd-George to Claud W. Sykes [copy]
Dr. M. Lothar to Dr. George Seybel (2)
E. St. Lo Malet to James Joyce (2) and to the Secretary of Incorporated Society of Authors (1)
Mary Kathleen Monaghan (Joyce) to George Harris Healey (6)
Mary Kathleen Monaghan (Joyce) to Stanislaus Joyce (13)
Mary Kathleen Monaghan (Joyce) to James Joyce (8)
Mary Kathleen Monaghan (Joyce) to Eileen Joyce
Mary Kathleen Monaghan (Joyce) to Nora Joyce
Mary Kathleen Monaghan (Joyce) to Ulick O'Connor [copy]
Mary Kathleen Monaghan (Joyce) to Israel Kaplan (13)
Carrol N. O'Sullivan to John S. Joyce (1) and to Dr. Birmingham (1)
James Brand Pinker to Nora Joyce
Ezra Loomis Pound to Nora Joyce (4)
Ezra Loomis Pound to Mrs. W. A. Bradley (2)
Ezra Loomis Pound to George H. Healey
The development of Joyce's image of women, MA Thesis, Valerie A. Purcel, Dec. 1984.
John Quinn to Ezra Loomis Pound (5)
John Quinn to Ben W. Huebsch [copy] (2)
Mrs. B. Richardson to Israel Kaplan
A Portrait of Oliver Gogarty, by W.R. Rodgers. Transcription of Radio transmission.
Attestation of graduation of James Joyce [copy]
Eileen Joyce Schaurek to Nora Joyce
Henry Blackman Sell to B. W. Huebsch
George Bernard Shaw to Sylvia Beach [copy]
Bill from Singer Societa for a sewing machine, 1910
Membership card, Societe Fraternelle d'Etudiants Protestants, John Millington Synge
letter of recommendation for James Joyce from Universita de Popolo (Trieste)
Those Longlong Words in Finnegans Wake, Joseph Vogel
Harriet Shaw Weaver to Nora Joyce
Herbert George Wells to Ezra Loomis Pound
Typescript of Wordsworth's "Tintern Abbey" bears a note in Stanislaus Joyce's hand
William Butler Yeats to Ezra Loomis Pound
Bills for piano moving and rental plus several statements from Luigi Zanoni

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Zurich. 1 leaf. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Photograph of James Joyce from N.Y. Post Library Review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New York. 1 leaf. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Zurich. 1 leaf. Verso: "Mrs. Nora Joyce in 'Riders to the Sea'." Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Scholes 1421.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Charles Joyce with his wife and his first son

scopecontent.p: 1 leaf. Scholes 1422.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Mrs. Charles Joyce, with small child, in front of a small frame house

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probably taken in the United States. 1 leaf. Scholes 1423.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Thomas M. Kettle, in legal robes and wig

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reproduced in Trieste, probably from a newspaper clipping. Not a good likeness according to J. F. Byrne. Scholes 1426.

did.unittitle: Group of photographs of homes of Joyce family in Dublin [4photographs]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dublin. 4 leaves. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Group of labeled envelopes describing contents of Joyce papers in possession of Mrs. Stanislaus Joyce. [8 envelopes]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 envelopes. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 leaves. 1 colored graph, not labeled; 1 note in pencil, unsigned, referring to "Giles" and "Mr M"; 1 sheet of Spanish poems, with translations into English, all in the same hand. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Photograph of 7 Eccles Street, Dublin, home of Joyce's friend J. F. Byrne and address of Leopold Bloom in Ulysses

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dublin. 1 leaf. Not in Scholes.

did.unittitle: Book review by Robert M. Adams of Nora: The Real Life of Molly Bloom, by Brenda Maddox [copy]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New York. 1 leaf. Not in Scholes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14 leaves bound. Photographs and commentary about film version of Ulysses. Not in Scholes.
